# Agent AHHOG — Output Formats

The default Agent AHHOG deliverable is a markdown report. Use the other formats when the user explicitly asks, or when context makes it obvious (e.g., "send this to Slack" → Slack format).

---

## Default: markdown report

Save to the user's working directory with filename `agent-ahhog-<workflow>-<domain>-<YYYY-MM-DD>.md`.

```markdown
# [Workflow name] — [target domain]
*Generated by Agent AHHOG · [YYYY-MM-DD] · Data source: Ahrefs MCP · Country: [country code]*

## TL;DR
- [Top finding #1 with the number]
- [Top finding #2 with the number]
- [Top finding #3 with the number]
- **Top action:** [one sentence on the single most important next step]

## Methodology
Pulled from Ahrefs MCP on [date]:
- `tool-name-1` for [target], [parameters]
- `tool-name-2` for [target], [parameters]
- ...

## Findings

### [Finding category 1]
[Substantive paragraph with the data woven in. Use a table when comparing.]

| Metric | Value | Source |
| --- | --- | --- |
| ... | ... | ... |

### [Finding category 2]
...

## Prioritized action list

1. **[Action]** — Target: [page/keyword/domain]. Expected impact: [traffic/links/etc.]. Effort: [S/M/L].
2. ...
3. ...

## Appendix: raw data
[Optional. Reference any CSV exports here.]
```

---

## Slack-ready summary

Short. Optimized for the channel feed. Use when the user says "post this to Slack" or "give me a Slack version".

If Slack MCP is connected and the user said "post to #channel" or similar: post the summary directly to the named channel, after confirming the channel if unspecified. Always also save the full markdown report locally — the Slack post links to or references it.

If Slack MCP is not connected: just output the summary as a text block the user can copy-paste.

Format:

```
*[Workflow name] · [target] · [date]*

*TL;DR:*
• [Finding 1]
• [Finding 2]
• [Finding 3]

*Top 3 actions:*
1. [Action]
2. [Action]
3. [Action]

Full report: [link or filename]
```

Use Slack-flavored markdown (asterisks for bold, no headers). Keep under ~15 lines.

---

## Notion page

Triggered when the user says "post to Notion", "make a Notion page", or similar. Requires Notion MCP to be connected.

If Notion MCP is connected:
- Create the page in the workspace/parent the user specifies, asking if it's ambiguous.
- Structure as: H1 title → callout block with metadata (date, source, country) → toggle per finding category → database/table for the action list.
- Pass the markdown content of the local report through — Notion accepts standard markdown for headings, lists, tables, code blocks, and bold/italic.

If Notion MCP is **not** connected and the user asked for Notion: tell them clearly, save the local markdown file, and note that they can paste it into Notion manually (Notion respects standard markdown when pasted).

---

## CSV export

For data-heavy workflows (link intersect prospects, broken link building list, keyword research output, programmatic SEO matrix), produce a CSV alongside the markdown report.

Filename pattern: `agent-ahhog-<workflow>-<domain>-<YYYY-MM-DD>.csv`.

Always include a header row. Standard columns by workflow:

- **link-intersect:** `domain,DR,competitors_linking_count,sample_linking_pages,outreach_angle`
- **broken-link-building:** `linking_domain,source_url,source_DR,broken_target,suggested_replacement,outreach_template`
- **content-gap:** `keyword,cluster,volume,KD,target_rank,competitor_best_rank,suggested_format`
- **keyword-research:** `keyword,cluster,volume,KD,CPC,intent,recommended_page_type`
- **content-decay:** `url,prior_traffic,current_traffic,pct_change,suspected_cause,recommended_action`
- **cannibalization:** `keyword,cluster,urls_competing,volume,best_rank,worst_rank,recommended_action`
- **migration-plan:** `source_url,target_url,traffic,backlinks,keywords,priority,notes`

---

## Linear / GitHub tickets

Triggered when the user says "open issues", "create tickets", "make tasks". Requires Linear MCP or GitHub MCP to be connected.

Create **one issue per top-priority action** from the action list — not one giant issue. Each issue:

- **Title:** the action verb-first ("Refresh /pricing page — declining traffic")
- **Description:** the relevant finding from the report, including the data points, the expected impact, and a link or reference to the full local report.
- **Labels:** workflow type (`seo`, `content`, `link-building`, `brand`, `technical-audit`) plus priority based on impact.
- **Project / repo:** confirm with the user if not obvious.

Don't create more than 10 issues in one batch without confirming — that's a lot of channel noise.

---

## Google Drive upload

Triggered when the user says "save to Drive", "upload to Google Docs", or similar. Requires Google Drive MCP to be connected.

Upload the markdown report as a Google Doc to the folder the user specifies (or Drive root if unspecified, after confirming). The local file remains as the canonical source.

---

## Dashboard (HTML / artifact)

For interactive output, produce a single-file HTML dashboard. Use it when the user asks for "a dashboard", "an interactive view", or "something I can share with the team".

Structure:
- Header with target, date, country
- KPI tiles for top-line metrics
- 2–4 charts (line for trends, bar for comparisons, table for the action list)
- Responsive, single-file, no external dependencies beyond CDN-hosted Chart.js or similar.

Save as `agent-ahhog-<workflow>-<domain>-<YYYY-MM-DD>.html`.

---

## Choosing the format

The local markdown report is **always produced** — that's the baseline. Beyond that:

- User said "Slack" / "channel" / "#xxx" → also post Slack summary (via Slack MCP if connected, else text block to copy)
- User said "Notion" / "page" / "doc for the team" → also create Notion page (via Notion MCP if connected, else note manual paste)
- User said "open issues" / "tickets" / "tasks" → also create issues (via Linear/GitHub MCP if connected, else list them in the report so user can create manually)
- User said "Drive" / "Google Doc" → also upload to Drive (via Drive MCP if connected)
- User said "dashboard" / "share with stakeholders" / "visualize" / "interactive" → also produce HTML dashboard
- Report contains a large table the user will filter/sort → also produce CSV (don't wait to be asked)

When in doubt, produce the markdown report only and offer the alternates: "Want me to also post this to Slack / create Notion page / open issues?"
